zh.lifeSummary: 當SIR THOMAS LORD BEVERSTONE KNIGHT ADAMS於1304年10月4日出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾貝福斯東時，他的父親Sir John William Ap Adam39歲，母親Lady Elizabeth De Gournai Beverstone29歲。他於1329年與Lady Jane Inge在英國藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1343年4月4日在他的家鄉享年38歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当SIR THOMAS LORD BEVERSTONE KNIGHT ADAMS于1304年10月4日出生在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾貝福斯東时，他的父亲Sir John William Ap Adam39岁，母亲Lady Elizabeth De Gournai Beverstone29岁。他于1329年与Lady Jane Inge在英国藍卡爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1343年4月4日在他的家乡享年38岁。

nameMeanings.name: ADAMS
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Dutch, and German (mainly northwestern Germany): patronymic from the personal name Adam . In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Greek Adamopoulos , Serbian and Croatian Adamović (see Adamovich ), Polish (and Jewish) Adamski .
Irish and Scottish: adopted for McAdam or a Scottish variant of Adam , with excrescent -s.

History: This surname was borne by two early presidents of the US, father and son. They were descended from Henry Adams, who settled in Braintree, MA, in 1635/6, from Barton St. David, Somerset, England. The younger of them, John Quincy Adams (1767–1848) derived his middle name from his maternal grandmother's surname (see   Quincy  ). — Another important New England family, established mainly in NH, is descended from William Adams, who emigrated from Shropshire, England, to Dedham, MA, in 1628. James Hopkins Adams (1812–61), governor of SC, was unconnected with either of these families, his ancestry being Welsh; his forebears entered North America through PA.

variants.name: Adam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Giuseppe, Santo, Giovanna, Nicola, Aldo, Baldassare, Carmelo, Carmino.
Italian: from the personal name Adamo, the Italian form of Adam .

nameMeanings.name: SIR
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘light; beauty; prosperity’, also ‘rank, power, royal majesty’, a byname of the goddesses Lakshmi and Saraswati. Also widely used as an honorific title.

nameMeanings.name: THOMAS
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: LORD
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Lorraine   or variant spelling of   Laurie .

variants.name: Lorraine
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin denoting a migrant from the province of Lorraine in eastern France. This derives its name from Latin Lotharingia ‘territory of the people of Lothar’. The latter is a Germanic personal name derived from hlud ‘fame’ + heri, hari ‘army’. Lorraine began to be used as a girl's name in Scotland in the 19th century, and for a time in the second half of the 20th century enjoyed great popularity, which has since waned.

nameMeanings.name: KNIGHT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

en.lifeSummary: When SIR THOMAS LORD BEVERSTONE KNIGHT ADAMS was born on 4 October 1304, in Beverston, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John William Ap Adam, was 39 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth De Gournai Beverstone, was 29. He married Lady Jane Inge in 1329, in Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 4 April 1343, in his hometown, at the age of 38.

Il a épousé Lady Jane Inge en 1329, à Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 4 avril 1343, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 38 ans.

Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Jane Inge в 1329 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 4 апреля 1343 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 38 лет.
